solution.p: We will factor 49r^{2}-4 where a=49, b=0, and c=-4.

Difference of Squares Formula: The formula for a difference of squares is a^2-b^2 = (a+b)(a-b) Note that the polynomial 49r^{2}-4 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]49r^{2}-4\amp =\left(7r\right)^2-\left(2\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(7r+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(7r-2\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (49) \cdot (-4) = -196.

The number -196 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-196)\amp -1(196)\amp 2(-98)\amp -2(98)
ewline4(-49)\amp -4(49)\amp 7(-28)\amp -7(28)
ewline14(-14)\amp -14(14)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 0, and this sum happens with (-14)+14=0 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]49r^{2}-4\amp =49r^{2}-14r+14r-4
ewline \amp =7r\mathopen{}\left(7r-2ight)+2\mathopen{}\left(7r-2ight)
ewline \amp =\left(7r+2ight)\mathopen{}\left(7r-2ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 49r^{2}-4 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 49, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(49)\amp 7(7)\amp \end{array}
The third term, -4, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-4)\amp -1(4)\amp 2(-2)\amp -2(2)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 49r^{2}-4 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]49r^{2}-4\amp =\left(7r+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(7r-2\right)\end{aligned}
